overview: This snake appears consistent with a White-banded Coralsnake (Micrurus albicinctus), a venomous species. It is characterized by a black body with white or yellow bands and small white spots. These snakes are typically found in South America, particularly in Brazil and Colombia. They are known to be nocturnal and semi-burrowing. iNaturalist +4 Disclaimer: Identifying snakes from images can be unreliable. Do not handle any wild snake, as many species are venomous and dangerous. 4 sites
